title: Bucatini All'Amatriciana
description: A classic Roman pasta with guanciale, tomatoes, and pecorino cheese. Simple, authentic, and absolutely delicious!
instructions: Slice guanciale or pancetta thinly, then cut into small pieces
Heat olive oil in a large pan and sauté guanciale until crispy, about 4 minutes
Add red pepper flakes and black pepper, stirring to combine
Add minced onion and garlic, cook for 8 minutes until softened
Add canned tomatoes (crushed by hand) and simmer for 15-20 minutes
Meanwhile, cook pasta 2 minutes before al dente
Transfer pasta directly to sauce with about 1/2 cup pasta water
Toss everything together and cook for 2 more minutes
Serve immediately with grated Pecorino Romano on top
tips: Guanciale is traditional but pancetta or bacon works too
Don't overcook the pasta - it finishes cooking in the sauce
Reserve pasta water to adjust sauce consistency
Use San Marzano tomatoes for best flavor
Pecorino Romano is essential - don't substitute with Parmesan
